title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables

input_row.title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables
input_row.description: Kubernetes supports mounting secrets as data volumes or as environment variables. Minimize the use of environment variable secrets.
input_row.rationale: It is reasonably common for application code to log out its environment (particularly in the event of an error). This will include any secret values passed in as environment variables, so secrets can easily be exposed to any user or entity who has access to the logs.
input_row.audit: Run the following command to find references to objects which use environment variables defined from secrets. kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A
input_row.remediation: If possible, rewrite application code to read secrets from mounted secret files, rather than from environment variables.
input_row.impact: Application code which expects to read secrets in the form of environment variables would need modification
input_row.default_value: By default, secrets are not defined
input_row.additional_information: Mounting secrets as volumes has the additional benefit that secret values can be updated without restarting the pod

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit command provided in the control can be executed using `kubectl`, which allows us to query the Kubernetes API for resources that use secrets as environment variables.
- The rationale indicates that logging environment variables can expose secrets, which reinforces the need to minimize their use.
- The control is focused on how secrets are utilized within Kubernetes resources, which can be verified through API calls.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_secrets_as_files_preferred

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A` to identify all Kubernetes resources that reference secrets as environment variables.
- This command will enumerate all resources across all namespaces that have a `secretKeyRef` in their configuration.
- The expected output should list the kinds of resources and their names that are using secrets as environment variables.
- **Pass Criteria:** If the output is empty, it indicates that no resources are using secrets as environment variables, which is compliant with the control.
- **Fail Criteria:** If any resources are listed in the output, it indicates non-compliance, as it shows that secrets are being used as environment variables instead of files.
